# opnsense_quagga_ospf6_network (Resource)

Configure OSPFv3 networks.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "opnsense_quagga_ospf6_network" "example" {
  ip_addr  = "2001:db8::"
  net_mask = "32"
  area     = "0.0.0.0"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `area` (String) The OSPFv3 area.
- `ip_addr` (String) The IPv6 network address.
- `net_mask` (String) The prefix length (0-128).

### Optional

- `area_range` (String) Area range for summarization. Defaults to `""`.
- `enabled` (Boolean) Enable this OSPFv3 network. Defaults to `true`.
- `prefix_list_in` (String) UUID of the prefix list for inbound direction. Defaults to `""`.
- `prefix_list_out` (String) UUID of the prefix list for outbound direction. Defaults to `""`.

### Read-Only

- `id` (String) UUID of the OSPFv3 network.

## Import

In Terraform v1.5.0 and later, use an [`import` block](https://developer.hashicorp.com/terraform/language/import) to import opnsense_quagga_ospf6_network using the `id`. For example:

```terraform
import {
  to = opnsense_quagga_ospf6_network.example
  id = "<opnsense-resource-id>"
}
```

Using `terraform import`, import opnsense_quagga_ospf6_network using the `id`. For example:

```console
% terraform import opnsense_quagga_ospf6_network.example <opnsense-resource-id>
```
